recommendations.episode: Why experts writing AI evals is creating the fastest-growing companies in history | Brendan Foody (CEO of Mercor)
recommendations.description: Brendan describes it as a phenomenal book on running companies.

recommendations.description: Provides incredible clarity on how to spend your time as a leader and manager. Jeff says it should be in every hotel room. It establishes a very high bar for evaluating work as the sum of everyone around you, clarifying that leadership is not an individual effort.

recommendations.episode: How Palantir built the ultimate founder factory | Nabeel S. Qureshi (founder, writer, ex-Palantir)
recommendations.description: A great business book where the most powerful aspect is understanding how Andy Grove thinks, not just the specific tactics. The actual book contains interesting stories and explanations that you don't get from reading summaries online.

recommendations.episode: Inside ChatGPT: The fastest-growing product in history | Nick Turley (Head of ChatGPT at OpenAI)
recommendations.description: Nick recommends this as a classic product management book that is extremely applicable in AI.

recommendations.episode: Driving alignment and urgency within teams, work-life balance, and the changing PM landscape | Nikita Miller (The Knot, Trello)
recommendations.description: Nikita recommends this as a software/management related book to other people.

recommendations.episode: 10 lessons on bootstrapping a $200m business | Patrick Campbell (ProfitWell)
recommendations.description: Patrick has read it probably 20 times in the past 10 years and reads it at least once a year. He's such a fan of Andy Grove that he commissioned a bronze bust of him.

recommendations.episode: Why LinkedIn is turning PMs into AI-powered \"full stack builders” | Tomer Cohen (LinkedIn CPO)
recommendations.description: Fundamental book for management with best practices that should help anyone start at a B level in managerial skills and then become an A over time.

recommendations.episode: How to make better decisions and build a joyful career
recommendations.description: Referenced by Lenny in the context of giving hard feedback with care and directness.

recommendations.episode: From startup to scaleup
recommendations.description: About the framework for giving hard feedback with care and directness.

recommendations.episode: Category creation and brand building | Barbra Gago (Pando, Miro, Greenhouse, Culture Amp)
recommendations.description: Really good for management and best practices around giving feedback. They give this out to customers.

recommendations.episode: An inside look at Figma’s unique GTM motion | Claire Butler (first GTM hire)
recommendations.description: Recommended for coaching new managers and helping them learn how to manage. One of the first two books she starts with because they're so good.

recommendations.episode: Why not asking for what you want is holding you back | Kenneth Berger (exec coach, first PM at Slack)
recommendations.description: A modern classic about challenging directly while caring personally, which aligns with speaking your truth while maintaining relationships.

recommendations.episode: The 10 traits of great PMs, how AI will impact your product, and Slack’s product development process | Noah Weiss (Slack, Foursquare, Google)
recommendations.description: Incredible book for those moving into leadership or management positions, and also really important for PMs doing soft influence.

recommendations.episode: Why experts writing AI evals is creating the fastest-growing companies in history | Brendan Foody (CEO of Mercor)
recommendations.description: Brendan finds it to be a really inspirational story.

recommendations.episode: Lessons from scaling Uber and Opendoor | Brian Tolkin (Head of Product at Opendoor, ex-Uber)
recommendations.description: One of Brian's most recommended books to other people.

recommendations.episode: Inside the expert network training every frontier AI model | Garrett Lord (Handshake CEO)
recommendations.description: Garrett thinks it's the epitome of starting a company.

recommendations.episode: “Dumbest idea I’ve heard” to $100M ARR: Inside the rise of Gamma | Grant Lee (CEO)
recommendations.description: Recommended for pre-product market fit founders. Teaches two key lessons: chase crazy ideas you're passionate about, and surround yourself with people who share that passion. Phil Knight focused on creating shoes for athletes because he was an athlete himself.

recommendations.episode: Mastering paid growth | Jonathan Becker (Thrive Digital)
recommendations.description: The startup story of Nike, talking about all the ups and downs. A fascinating read about taking risk and doing what you think is right and ultimately succeeding.

recommendations.episode: The non-technical PM’s guide to building with Cursor | Zevi Arnovitz (Meta)
recommendations.description: The Nike story, one of Zevi's favorite business books.

books.author: N/A

recommendations.episode: Becoming more strategic, navigating difficult colleagues, harnessing founder mode, and more | Anneka Gupta (Chief Product Officer at Rubrik)
recommendations.description: Great book about mindset and how to approach hard things.

recommendations.episode: The essence of product management | Christian Idiodi (SVPG)
recommendations.description: Describes the mindset and culture that good product work is done in.

recommendations.episode: Inside the expert network training every frontier AI model | Garrett Lord (Handshake CEO)
recommendations.description: Garrett mentions this as one of the books he recommends, noting it's a common but classic book about entrepreneurship.

recommendations.episode: Building a meaningful career | Jason Shah (Airbnb, Amazon, Microsoft, Alchemy)
recommendations.description: Teaches product managers to chew glass and care about outcomes the way that a CEO has to, which is a really useful mindset to have.

recommendations.episode: Frameworks for product differentiation, team building, and thinking from first principles | Ayo Omojola (Carbon Health, Cash App)
recommendations.description: Recommended as one of Ayo's top book recommendations. It's a sci-fi book series where the first book is considered the worst in the series.

recommendations.episode: Monetizing passions, scaling marketplaces, and stories from a creator economy vet | Camille Hearst (Spotify, Patreon, Apple, YouTube)
recommendations.description: Recommended by Camille as one of her top book recommendations. A sci-fi book that is getting adapted into a show.

recommendations.episode: How we restructured Airtable’s entire org for AI | Howie Liu (co-founder and CEO)
recommendations.description: A mind-expanding sci-fi book that opens your brain. It's actually a three-book series that Howie recommends for anyone who hasn't read it. Each subsequent book is like dropping into another layer.

recommendations.episode: How to measure AI developer productivity in 2025 | Nicole Forsgren
recommendations.description: Lenny describes it as the best sci-fi ever and mentions they're making a movie of it. Nicole says it's on her list.

recommendations.episode: Billion dollar failures, and billion dollar success | Tom Conrad (Quibi, Pandora, Pets.com, Snap, Zero)
recommendations.description: Mentioned by Lenny as a complicated but incredible book that has come up a couple of times on the podcast.

recommendations.episode: Brian Chesky's secret mentor who died 9 times, started the Burning Man board, and built the world's first midlife wisdom school | Chip Conley (founder of MEA)
recommendations.description: Chip's favorite book of all time, written by Viktor Frankl about his experience in a concentration camp in World War II. He recommends it to people going through hard times, saying it helps put things in perspective and speaks to the idea that despair equals suffering minus meaning.

recommendations.episode: Scaling Duolingo, embracing failure, and insight into Latin America’s tech scene | Gina Gotthilf (Latitud, Duolingo)
recommendations.description: Gina recommends this because she's a philosopher at heart and thinks a lot about life and the meaning of life. She believes it's important to think about how to live a good life no matter what profession you choose.

recommendations.episode: Becoming a conscious leader: Leading without fear, finding your life’s objective function, and getting better at vision and strategy | John Mark Nickels (Uber, Waymo, DoorDash)
recommendations.description: John Mark uses this as an example of taking responsibility and showing compassion even in the most oppressive situations, like Frankl did in concentration camps. He says if Frankl could show empathy for his oppressors in those conditions, we can show up differently in our daily challenges.

recommendations.episode: How to show up in any room with a low heart rate: Silicon Valley’s missing etiquette playbook | Sam Lessin
recommendations.description: Sam describes it as a great book worth reading.

recommendations.episode: How to grow a subscription business | Yuriy Timen (Grammarly, Canva, Airtable)
recommendations.description: A remarkable memoir on perseverance with the key takeaway that you can't control what's happening around you, but you can control your reaction to it.

recommendations.episode: The essence of product management | Christian Idiodi (SVPG)
recommendations.description: Reflects decades of love and passion for product work and describes the heart of good product work. Written after going through failures in product management.

recommendations.episode: Understanding the role of product ops | Christine Itwaru (Pendo)
recommendations.description: A classic that helped Christine fall in love with product. She finds it inspirational.

recommendations.episode: AI and product management | Marily Nika (Meta, Google)
recommendations.description: A book about how to create tech products people love.

recommendations.episode: Lessons from Atlassian: Launching new products, getting buy-in, and staying ahead of the competition | Megan Cook (head of product, Jira)
recommendations.description: Megan sends this most often to new PMs especially. It holds up really well and has great foundational knowledge and tips for product managers.

recommendations.episode: How to ask the right questions, project confidence, and win over skeptics | Paige Costello (Asana, Intercom, Intuit)
recommendations.description: Paige's go-to book recommendation for other PMs, which she describes as a classic.

recommendations.episode: Lessons from scaling Stripe | Claire Hughes Johnson (former COO of Stripe)
recommendations.description: Claire's own book about operations and management. Lenny describes it as 'exactly my newsletter, but as a book about operations' and recommends it to anyone listening.

recommendations.episode: Bending the universe in your favor | Claire Vo (LaunchDarkly, Color, Optimizely, ChatPRD)
recommendations.description: Has solid playbook answers to 80-90% of everyday leadership scaling people questions. Solves things that you don't need to solve novel-ly.

recommendations.episode: How to foster innovation and big thinking | Eeke de Milliano (Retool, Stripe)
recommendations.description: Eeke helped with early drafts as a ghost writer. He still uses tactics from the early chapters in management and recommends them.

recommendations.episode: Mastering product strategy and growing as a PM | Maggie Crowley (Toast, Drift, Tripadvisor)
recommendations.description: A desk reference that sits on her desk and she references regularly, especially as a manager.

recommendations.episode: Lessons from Atlassian: Launching new products, getting buy-in, and staying ahead of the competition | Megan Cook (head of product, Jira)
recommendations.description: Megan sends this to her managers. It has an incredible amount of really useful tactical things, templates, and practical advice that can be put into practice immediately.

recommendations.episode: AI platforms at Microsoft
recommendations.description: About treating the cause not the symptoms.

recommendations.episode: LinkedIn’s product evolution and the art of building complex systems | Hari Srinivasan (LinkedIn)
recommendations.description: Hari gives this to his team regularly. It provides detailed insight into how people can intervene at various parts of systems and how to take actions at different systems.

recommendations.episode: 10 contrarian leadership truths every leader needs to hear | Matt MacInnis (Rippling)
recommendations.description: The best generic framework for thinking about how systems work. You will extrapolate from this book to every aspect of your life after reading it.

recommendations.description: Nickey's favorite book that she recommends most to other product managers.

recommendations.episode: The ultimate guide to AEO: How to get ChatGPT to recommend your product | Ethan Smith (Graphite)

recommendations.episode: Inside ChatGPT: The fastest-growing product in history | Nick Turley (Head of ChatGPT at OpenAI)
recommendations.description: Nick recommends this as a classic product management book that is extremely applicable in AI.

recommendations.episode: Vision, conviction, and hype: How to build 0 to 1 inside a company | Mihika Kapoor (Product at Figma)

recommendations.episode: The hierarchy of engagement | Sarah Tavel (Benchmark, Greylock, Pinterest)

recommendations.episode: How to measure AI developer productivity in 2025 | Nicole Forsgren
recommendations.description: One of Nicole's top book recommendations.

recommendations.episode: A guide to difficult conversations, building high-trust teams, and designing a life you love | Rachel Lockett
recommendations.description: Rachel loves this book and recommends it frequently. Lenny agrees that people don't talk about this book enough.

Patrick Collison credited this book with helping build Stripe.
